rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical vicinal oxysulfonylation of olefins with sulfinic acids and O-nucleophiles (water, alcohols, or carboxylic acids) to give β-hydroxy/alkoxy/acyloxy sulfones and β-sulfonyl lactones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfinic acid (PhSO2H) to sulfonyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic radical intermediate to a benzylic cation; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical from sulfonyl addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation after second anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode first oxidizes sulfinic acid to a sulfonyl radical (I), which adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical (II); a second anodic SET converts II to a benzylic cation (III), which is trapped by an O-nucleophile (H2O, ROH, or RCO2H) to deliver the β-oxysulfone, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
notes: Mechanism proposed based on relative redox potentials (styrene higher than sulfinic acid) rather than radical-trap or DFT experiments. X-ray structure (CCDC 1893818) of product 3b confirms regiochemistry. Intramolecular variant with tethered alcohol/carboxylic acid gives cyclic ethers and lactones, consistent with cation trapping by internal nucleophile.
